title: erigeron acris subsp. angulosus
intro: Erigeron acris subsp. angulosus, also known as the tall fleabane or tall daisy, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Erigeron acris subsp. angulosus include its potential to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Erigeron acris subsp. angulosus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
